cityPage.title: Planetary Hours in {city} Today
cityPage.descriptionTemplate: In {city}, {country}, planetary hours are calculated using local coordinates ({lat}, {lon}) and timezone {timezone}, ensuring accurate sunrise and sunset based timing.
cityPage.customLocation: Want to calculate planetary hours for a custom location?

faqTemplates.todayQuestion: What are the planetary hours in {city} today?
faqTemplates.todayAnswer: Today, planetary hours in {city} begin at sunrise ({sunrise}) and are ruled by {dayRuler}. Each daytime planetary hour lasts about {dayMinutes} minutes, while each nighttime hour lasts about {nightMinutes} minutes.
faqTemplates.rulerAnswer: The day ruler in {city} today is {dayRuler}. The day ruler is the planet that governs the first planetary hour after sunrise. In the Chaldean order, each day of the week is ruled by a specific planet: Sun (Sunday), Moon (Monday), Mars (Tuesday), Mercury (Wednesday), Jupiter (Thursday), Venus (Friday), and Saturn (Saturday).
faqTemplates.calculationQuestion: How are planetary hours calculated for {city}?
faqTemplates.calculationAnswer: Planetary hours for {city} are calculated using the city's precise coordinates ({lat}, {lon}) and timezone ({timezone}). The time between sunrise and sunset is divided into 12 equal daytime planetary hours, and the time between sunset and the next sunrise is divided into 12 equal nighttime hours. Each hour is assigned a planet following the Chaldean order.

about.title: About Planetary Hours
about.description: Learn about the mission, methodology and team behind the Planetary Hours Calculator.
about.whatArePHText: Planetary hours are an ancient astrological system that divides the time between sunrise and sunset, and between sunset and sunrise, into 12 unequal "hours." Each hour is ruled by one of the seven traditional planets (Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn).
about.chaldeanOrderText: The sequence of rulers follows the Chaldean order of the planets, based on their apparent speed from Earth's perspective. The planet ruling the first hour of the day (starting at sunrise) is the ruler of that day.
about.howCanBeUsedText: Practitioners believe that the nature of the ruling planet influences the energy and suitability of that hour for certain activities.

planetHourExamples.sun: Sun Hour: Good for activities related to leadership, vitality, success, and seeking favor from authorities.
planetHourExamples.venus: Venus Hour: Favors love, beauty, art, social activities, and pleasure.
planetHourExamples.mercury: Mercury Hour: Suitable for communication, learning, writing, trade, and travel.
planetHourExamples.moon: Moon Hour: Related to domestic matters, emotions, intuition, and public affairs.
planetHourExamples.saturn: Saturn Hour: Associated with discipline, responsibility, long-term projects, dealing with obstacles, and endings.
planetHourExamples.jupiter: Jupiter Hour: Ideal for growth, expansion, abundance, seeking wisdom, and financial matters.
planetHourExamples.mars: Mars Hour: Connected to energy, courage, action, competition, and potential conflict.

keyFeaturesItems.locationInput: Location input with Google Places auto-complete or one-click geolocation.
keyFeaturesItems.timezoneHandling: Automatic timezone handling to ensure accurate sunrise/sunset and hour calculations.
keyFeaturesItems.weekNavigation: Week navigation to quickly jump to any day.
keyFeaturesItems.contextualSuggestions: Contextual suggestions on what activities are good for or should be avoided during each hour.

about.limitationsText: Planetary hours originate from traditional astrology. The calculations provided here are based on widely accepted historical methods and modern astronomical data for sunrise and sunset. They are offered for informational and entertainment purposes only and should not replace professional advice or planning.
about.closingText: This calculator helps you determine planetary hours for any date and place, empowering you to schedule tasks in harmony with the prevailing planetary energies.
